title: davallia speluncae
intro: Davallia speluncae, also known as the cave fern or stone brake, is a type of flowering plant in the family Davalliaceae, native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Davallia speluncae include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties, and it can also be dried and used in capsules or supplements.
